overview: The Component Object Model (COM) is a Windows framework that allows software components to communicate and create dynamic objects. COM objects are often implemented as DLLs and registered in the Windows Registry with unique Class Identifiers (CLSID). workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
